observations: GET /v1/remediation/:id/proposal returns HTTP 200 with the plan listing AWS actions (steps with action, label, description, riskLevel, params) — verified against running API on port 3099 (mapped from container port 5171).
POST /v1/remediation/:id/approve with admin JWT returns HTTP 200 and sets remediation status to 'approved' — approvedBy field is populated from JWT email claim.
POST /v1/remediation/:id/approve a second time returns HTTP 409 with error code CONFLICT — the ApproveRemediationUseCase correctly rejects when status is not 'proposed'.

ApproveRemediationUseCase creates an ApprovalEntity via IApprovalRepository.create() — confirmed by code audit of approve-remediation.usecase.ts which constructs and persists a PendingApproval record (stored in-memory via PgApprovalRepository in OSS runtime).
The dist image was stale (built from commit 87cb2602 before the AC-023 routes and approve logic). Rebuilt locally and copied into the running container. The running dist now matches HEAD.

A TypeScript build error in approve-remediation.usecase.ts was fixed: plain string assignments to ApprovalId and NotificationId branded types were replaced with approvalId() and notificationId() helper calls.
RBAC enforcement: the approve route requires admin role via requireRole('admin') middleware.
Proposal route returns all required fields: remediationId, incidentId, status, rootCause, description, steps (action + params), proposedBy, createdAt, updatedAt.

RE-VERIFIED at HTTP boundary on port 5171 (OSS runtime). Created incident, proposed remediation, called GET /api/v1/remediation/:id/proposal → plan with AWS actions, POST /api/v1/remediation/:id/approve with admin JWT → 200 + status=approved + approvedBy populated, second approve → HTTP 409 CONFLICT. All three AC-023 checks pass against real API.

Attempt 3 verify-first (coding-agent): API healthy at host :3099 (container PORT=5171). Zero-diff re-verification — GET proposal 200 with ecs.updateService/ssm.runCommand steps, POST approve 200 approvedBy=admin@causeflow.ai, second POST approve 409 CONFLICT (status completed after auto-execute). implementation=true.
